RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"
cat >&2 <<'BANNER'
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"
            info "Linux detected ($PRETTY_NAME)"
        else
            DISTRO="unknown"
            warn "Linux detected but /etc/os-release missing — some installs may fail"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        sudo apt-get update -qq
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
        (type -p wget >/dev/null || (sudo apt update && sudo apt-get install wget -y))
        sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
        out=$(mktemp)
        wget -qO "$out" https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg < "$out" >/dev/null
        rm -f "$out"
        sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | \
            sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list >/dev/null
        sudo apt-get update -qq
        sudo apt-get install -y gh
        info "gh installed via apt"
    else
        fail "Cannot auto-install gh on this distro — install manually: https://cli.github.com"
    fi
fi

# ── GitHub Authentication ───────────────────────────────────
step "Authenticating with GitHub"

if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
    info "Already authenticated with GitHub"
else
    printf >&2 "  You'll be prompted to log in. Choose ${BOLD}HTTPS${RESET} and follow the\n"
    printf >&2 "  browser flow, or paste a personal access token.\n\n"
    gh auth login
    if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
        info "GitHub authentication successful"
    else
        fail "GitHub authentication failed — re-run this script to try again"
    fi
fi

# ── Clone Repository ───────────────────────────────────────
step "Cloning repository"

if [[ -d "$RUNE_DIR/.git" ]]; then
    info "Repo already exists at $RUNE_DIR — pulling latest..."
    git -C "$RUNE_DIR" pull --ff-only || warn "Pull failed (you may have local changes) — continuing with existing code"
else
    gh repo clone "$REPO" "$RUNE_DIR"
    info "Cloned to $RUNE_DIR"
fi

cd "$RUNE_DIR"

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

uv sync
info "All workspace dependencies installed"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

if uv run python -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if uv run python -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else
    warn "inference library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then
    info "All smoke tests passed"
fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then
    GPU_INFO=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$GPU_INFO" ]]; then
        info "NVIDIA GPU detected: $GPU_INFO"
    fi
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
        warn "nvidia-smi not found — GPU training/inference requires NVIDIA drivers + CUDA"
    fi
fi

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    Rune is ready!   ${RUNE_DIR}
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DONE
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  cd ${RUNE_DIR}

  Run tests:       uv run pytest
  Lint:            uv run ruff check .
  Type check:      uv run mypy libs/ services/ scripts/ --ignore-missing-imports
  Format:          uv run ruff format .
  Full stack:      docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build

DONE

content.text:
Done. `setup.sh` is at the repo root and handles:

1. **Banner + confirmation** — shows what it'll do, prompts before proceeding (auto-skips when piped)
2. **OS detection** — macOS or Ubuntu/Debian
3. **System packages** — Homebrew on macOS, apt on Debian/Ubuntu (idempotent)
4. **GitHub CLI** — installs `gh` via brew or official apt repo
5. **GitHub auth** — `gh auth login` with friendly instructions (skips if already authed)
6. **Clone** — `gh repo clone` to `$RUNE_DIR` (default `~/rune`), or pulls if already cloned
7. **uv + Python 3.12** — installs uv, then `uv python install 3.12` + `uv sync`
8. **Smoke tests** — verifies core imports work
9. **Advisory checks** — Docker and NVIDIA GPU status (non-fatal)
10. **Next-steps summary**

Override install location with `RUNE_DIR=~/wherever bash setup.sh`. Disable colors with `NO_COLOR=1`.

content: I got this when I ran the script: E: The repository 'https://deb.debian.org/debian bullseye-backports Release' no longer has a Release file.

message.content: I got this when I ran the script: E: The repository 'https://deb.debian.org/debian bullseye-backports Release' no longer has a Release file.

content.text:
That's a stale apt sources issue on your instance (Debian Bullseye backports repo was archived). It's not caused by the script — it happens on any `apt-get update`. But the script should handle it gracefully rather than dying.

Two fixes:

1. **Comment out the dead backports source before running apt**
2. **Use `apt-get update || true`** so a partial repo failure doesn't abort the script

I'll go with option 1 (actually fix the problem) plus a fallback:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  Rune — one-command dev environment setup
#
#  Usage:
#    bash setup.sh                          # interactive
#    RUNE_DIR=~/projects/rune bash setup.sh # custom location
#    curl -fsSL <raw-url>/setup.sh | bash   # remote (non-interactive)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO="ElixirTrials/rune"
RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"
cat >&2 <<'BANNER'
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"
            info "Linux detected ($PRETTY_NAME)"
        else
            DISTRO="unknown"
            warn "Linux detected but /etc/os-release missing — some installs may fail"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        sudo apt-get update -qq
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
        (type -p wget >/dev/null || (sudo apt update && sudo apt-get install wget -y))
        sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
        out=$(mktemp)
        wget -qO "$out" https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg < "$out" >/dev/null
        rm -f "$out"
        sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | \
            sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list >/dev/null
        sudo apt-get update -qq
        sudo apt-get install -y gh
        info "gh installed via apt"
    else
        fail "Cannot auto-install gh on this distro — install manually: https://cli.github.com"
    fi
fi

# ── GitHub Authentication ───────────────────────────────────
step "Authenticating with GitHub"

if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
    info "Already authenticated with GitHub"
else
    printf >&2 "  You'll be prompted to log in. Choose ${BOLD}HTTPS${RESET} and follow the\n"
    printf >&2 "  browser flow, or paste a personal access token.\n\n"
    gh auth login
    if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
        info "GitHub authentication successful"
    else
        fail "GitHub authentication failed — re-run this script to try again"
    fi
fi

# ── Clone Repository ───────────────────────────────────────
step "Cloning repository"

if [[ -d "$RUNE_DIR/.git" ]]; then
    info "Repo already exists at $RUNE_DIR — pulling latest..."
    git -C "$RUNE_DIR" pull --ff-only || warn "Pull failed (you may have local changes) — continuing with existing code"
else
    gh repo clone "$REPO" "$RUNE_DIR"
    info "Cloned to $RUNE_DIR"
fi

cd "$RUNE_DIR"

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

uv sync
info "All workspace dependencies installed"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

if uv run python -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if uv run python -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else
    warn "inference library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then
    info "All smoke tests passed"
fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then
    GPU_INFO=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$GPU_INFO" ]]; then
        info "NVIDIA GPU detected: $GPU_INFO"
    fi
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
        warn "nvidia-smi not found — GPU training/inference requires NVIDIA drivers + CUDA"
    fi
fi

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    Rune is ready!   ${RUNE_DIR}
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DONE
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  cd ${RUNE_DIR}

  Run tests:       uv run pytest
  Lint:            uv run ruff check .
  Type check:      uv run mypy libs/ services/ scripts/ --ignore-missing-imports
  Format:          uv run ruff format .
  Full stack:      docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build

DONE

content.text: Good, the script is at `scripts/setup.sh`. The fix: before running `apt-get update`, disable any stale sources that would cause it to fail. I'll add a helper that comments out dead repos and use `|| true` as a fallback:

input.old_string:
elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else

        fi

input.new_string:
elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev wget; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        # Disable stale apt sources (e.g. archived bullseye-backports) that
        # would make apt-get update fail with "no longer has a Release file".
        fix_stale_apt_sources() {
            local src_dir="/etc/apt/sources.list.d"
            for f in /etc/apt/sources.list "$src_dir"/*.list; do
                [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
                if grep -qE '(bullseye-backports|stretch|jessie)' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
                    warn "Disabling stale apt source: $f"
                    sudo sed -i.bak '/bullseye-backports\|stretch\|jessie/s/^/#/' "$f"
                fi
            done
        }
        fix_stale_apt_sources

        if ! sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null; then
            warn "apt-get update had errors — retrying with --allow-releaseinfo-change..."
            sudo apt-get update -qq --allow-releaseinfo-change || warn "apt-get update partially failed — continuing anyway"
        fi
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  Rune — one-command dev environment setup
#
#  Usage:
#    bash setup.sh                          # interactive
#    RUNE_DIR=~/projects/rune bash setup.sh # custom location
#    curl -fsSL <raw-url>/setup.sh | bash   # remote (non-interactive)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO="ElixirTrials/rune"
RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"

        else
            DISTRO="unknown"

        fi

    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then

        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        sudo apt-get update -qq
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"

    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then

step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then

else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

uv sync
info "All workspace dependencies installed"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

if uv run python -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then

else

    SMOKE_PASS=false

if uv run python -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then

fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then

else

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  Rune — one-command dev environment setup
#
#  Usage:
#    bash setup.sh                          # interactive
#    RUNE_DIR=~/projects/rune bash setup.sh # custom location
#    curl -fsSL <raw-url>/setup.sh | bash   # remote (non-interactive)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO="ElixirTrials/rune"
RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"
cat >&2 <<'BANNER'
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"
            info "Linux detected ($PRETTY_NAME)"
        else
            DISTRO="unknown"
            warn "Linux detected but /etc/os-release missing — some installs may fail"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev wget; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        # Disable stale apt sources (e.g. archived bullseye-backports) that
        # would make apt-get update fail with "no longer has a Release file".
        fix_stale_apt_sources() {
            local src_dir="/etc/apt/sources.list.d"
            for f in /etc/apt/sources.list "$src_dir"/*.list; do
                [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
                if grep -qE '(bullseye-backports|stretch|jessie)' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
                    warn "Disabling stale apt source: $f"
                    sudo sed -i.bak '/bullseye-backports\|stretch\|jessie/s/^/#/' "$f"
                fi
            done
        }
        fix_stale_apt_sources

        if ! sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null; then
            warn "apt-get update had errors — retrying with --allow-releaseinfo-change..."
            sudo apt-get update -qq --allow-releaseinfo-change || warn "apt-get update partially failed — continuing anyway"
        fi
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
        (type -p wget >/dev/null || (sudo apt update && sudo apt-get install wget -y))
        sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
        out=$(mktemp)
        wget -qO "$out" https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg < "$out" >/dev/null
        rm -f "$out"
        sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | \
            sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list >/dev/null
        sudo apt-get update -qq
        sudo apt-get install -y gh
        info "gh installed via apt"
    else
        fail "Cannot auto-install gh on this distro — install manually: https://cli.github.com"
    fi
fi

# ── GitHub Authentication ───────────────────────────────────
step "Authenticating with GitHub"

if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
    info "Already authenticated with GitHub"
else
    printf >&2 "  You'll be prompted to log in. Choose ${BOLD}HTTPS${RESET} and follow the\n"
    printf >&2 "  browser flow, or paste a personal access token.\n\n"
    gh auth login
    if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
        info "GitHub authentication successful"
    else
        fail "GitHub authentication failed — re-run this script to try again"
    fi
fi

# ── Clone Repository ───────────────────────────────────────
step "Cloning repository"

if [[ -d "$RUNE_DIR/.git" ]]; then
    info "Repo already exists at $RUNE_DIR — pulling latest..."
    git -C "$RUNE_DIR" pull --ff-only || warn "Pull failed (you may have local changes) — continuing with existing code"
else
    gh repo clone "$REPO" "$RUNE_DIR"
    info "Cloned to $RUNE_DIR"
fi

cd "$RUNE_DIR"

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

uv sync
info "All workspace dependencies installed"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

if uv run python -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if uv run python -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else
    warn "inference library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then
    info "All smoke tests passed"
fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then
    GPU_INFO=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$GPU_INFO" ]]; then
        info "NVIDIA GPU detected: $GPU_INFO"
    fi
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
        warn "nvidia-smi not found — GPU training/inference requires NVIDIA drivers + CUDA"
    fi
fi

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    Rune is ready!   ${RUNE_DIR}
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DONE
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  cd ${RUNE_DIR}

  Run tests:       uv run pytest
  Lint:            uv run ruff check .
  Type check:      uv run mypy libs/ services/ scripts/ --ignore-missing-imports
  Format:          uv run ruff format .
  Full stack:      docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build

DONE

content.text:
Here's what the fix does:

1. **`fix_stale_apt_sources()`** — before `apt-get update`, scans `/etc/apt/sources.list` and `sources.list.d/*.list` for known-dead repos (bullseye-backports, stretch, jessie) and comments them out (backs up originals as `.bak`)
2. **Fallback retry** — if `apt-get update` still fails, retries with `--allow-releaseinfo-change`, and if that fails too, continues anyway (the packages we need are in the main repos)
3. **gh install** — switched from `wget` to `curl` (already guaranteed installed) and added `|| true` to its own `apt-get update` call
4. **Added `wget`** to the system packages list since it's useful and wasn't there before

Try running it again — it should get past the stale repo error now.

content.text: That's not a GPU issue — the workspace packages aren't on `sys.path` for a bare `python -c`. `uv run` sets up the venv but these are editable workspace members that need the venv's installed paths. Let me check what `uv run python` actually resolves to:

input.command: uv run python -c "import sys; print('\n'.join(p for p in sys.path if 'rune' in p))"

content.text:
Works fine here because `uv sync` installed the editable packages. On their machine, the workspace packages aren't being found. This likely means `uv sync` didn't complete cleanly — perhaps it failed partway through due to GPU deps like `torch` that can't install on their machine.

The fix: use `uv run --no-sync` so it doesn't try to reinstall, and more importantly, make the smoke test actually test what matters (that the venv exists and has the workspace packages) rather than something fragile. Let me also check what happens on a CPU-only install:

content.content: /Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/shared/src/shared/__init__.py

content.text:
Works here. The issue on their machine is most likely that `uv sync` partially failed (e.g. `torch` or `bitsandbytes` can't build) and the workspace packages didn't get installed. The script showed `[OK] All workspace dependencies installed` but `uv sync` may have exited 0 despite warnings.

Let me fix the smoke tests to be more diagnostic, and also make `uv sync` more resilient:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  Rune — one-command dev environment setup
#
#  Usage:
#    bash setup.sh                          # interactive
#    RUNE_DIR=~/projects/rune bash setup.sh # custom location
#    curl -fsSL <raw-url>/setup.sh | bash   # remote (non-interactive)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO="ElixirTrials/rune"
RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"
cat >&2 <<'BANNER'
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"
            info "Linux detected ($PRETTY_NAME)"
        else
            DISTRO="unknown"
            warn "Linux detected but /etc/os-release missing — some installs may fail"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev wget; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        # Disable stale apt sources (e.g. archived bullseye-backports) that
        # would make apt-get update fail with "no longer has a Release file".
        fix_stale_apt_sources() {
            local src_dir="/etc/apt/sources.list.d"
            for f in /etc/apt/sources.list "$src_dir"/*.list; do
                [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
                if grep -qE '(bullseye-backports|stretch|jessie)' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
                    warn "Disabling stale apt source: $f"
                    sudo sed -i.bak '/bullseye-backports\|stretch\|jessie/s/^/#/' "$f"
                fi
            done
        }
        fix_stale_apt_sources

        if ! sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null; then
            warn "apt-get update had errors — retrying with --allow-releaseinfo-change..."
            sudo apt-get update -qq --allow-releaseinfo-change || warn "apt-get update partially failed — continuing anyway"
        fi
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
        sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
        curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
            | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg >/dev/null
        sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | \
            sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list >/dev/null
        sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null || true
        sudo apt-get install -y gh
        info "gh installed via apt"
    else
        fail "Cannot auto-install gh on this distro — install manually: https://cli.github.com"
    fi
fi

# ── GitHub Authentication ───────────────────────────────────
step "Authenticating with GitHub"

if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
    info "Already authenticated with GitHub"
else
    printf >&2 "  You'll be prompted to log in. Choose ${BOLD}HTTPS${RESET} and follow the\n"
    printf >&2 "  browser flow, or paste a personal access token.\n\n"
    gh auth login
    if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
        info "GitHub authentication successful"
    else
        fail "GitHub authentication failed — re-run this script to try again"
    fi
fi

# ── Clone Repository ───────────────────────────────────────
step "Cloning repository"

if [[ -d "$RUNE_DIR/.git" ]]; then
    info "Repo already exists at $RUNE_DIR — pulling latest..."
    git -C "$RUNE_DIR" pull --ff-only || warn "Pull failed (you may have local changes) — continuing with existing code"
else
    gh repo clone "$REPO" "$RUNE_DIR"
    info "Cloned to $RUNE_DIR"
fi

cd "$RUNE_DIR"

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

uv sync
info "All workspace dependencies installed"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

if uv run python -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if uv run python -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else
    warn "inference library import failed (may need GPU deps)"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then
    info "All smoke tests passed"
fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then
    GPU_INFO=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$GPU_INFO" ]]; then
        info "NVIDIA GPU detected: $GPU_INFO"
    fi
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
        warn "nvidia-smi not found — GPU training/inference requires NVIDIA drivers + CUDA"
    fi
fi

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    Rune is ready!   ${RUNE_DIR}
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DONE
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  cd ${RUNE_DIR}

  Run tests:       uv run pytest
  Lint:            uv run ruff check .
  Type check:      uv run mypy libs/ services/ scripts/ --ignore-missing-imports
  Format:          uv run ruff format .
  Full stack:      docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build

DONE

The real question is: **did `uv sync` actually succeed on your machine?** The `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'shared'` means the workspace packages didn't get installed into the venv. 

Can you check: after running the script, does `~/rune/.venv/` exist? And what does `uv sync` output when you run it manually from `~/rune`? That'll tell us if it's a partial install failure (likely from `torch`/`bitsandbytes` not building on your instance).

[project]
name = "elixirtrials--template"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Template Repo ElixirTrials "
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "aiohttp>=3.10.0",
    "httpx>=0.28.1",
    "markdown>=3.4.0",
    "playwright>=1.40.0",
    "mkdocs>=1.6.1",
    "mkdocs-monorepo-plugin>=1.0.0",
    "mkdocstrings[python]>=0.24.0",
    "requests>=2.32.5",
    "tenacity>=8.2.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=1.3.0",
    "aioresponses>=0.7.8",
    "fastapi>=0.128.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0.45",
    "sqlmodel>=0.0.31",
    "python-dotenv>=1.2.1",
    "diskcache>=5.6.3",
    "platformdirs>=4.5.1",
    "langchain>=1.2.6",
    "langgraph>=1.0.6",
    "python-multipart>=0.0.21",
    "alembic>=1.13.0",
    "pymdown-extensions>=10.21",
    "openai>=1.0.0",
    "psutil>=5.9.0",
    "llama-cpp-python>=0.3.0",
]

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "mypy>=1.19.0",
    "pytest>=9.0.2",
    "pytest-asyncio>=1.2.0",
    "pytest-cov>=6.0.0",
    "ruff>=0.14.8",
    "aioresponses>=0.7.8",
    "mlflow>=3.8.1",
    "pytest-xdist>=3.8.0",
    "requests-mock>=1.12.1",
]

[tool.uv.workspace]
members = [
    "services/api-service",
    "services/evolution-svc",
    "services/rune-agent",
    "services/training-svc",
    "libs/evaluation",
    "libs/events-py",
    "libs/inference",
    "libs/model-training",
    "libs/shared",
    "libs/adapter-registry",
]

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py312"
line-length = 88
exclude = ["venv", ".venv", "docs", "site", ".planning"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "W", "C", "N", "I", "D"]
ignore = ["E203", "D203", "D213", "D100", "D413", "D104"]
pydocstyle.convention = "google"

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"tests/**" = ["D"]
"**/tests/**" = ["D"]
"conftest.py" = ["D"]
"**/conftest.py" = ["D"]
"scripts/**" = ["D", "C901", "E501", "E402"]
"**/scripts/**" = ["D", "C901", "E501", "E402"]

[tool.mypy]
exclude = "^(site|services/.*/tests|libs/.*/tests|instructions|notebooks)/"

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "fastapi"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "pydantic"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "yaml"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "sqlalchemy"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "sqlalchemy.engine"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "sqlmodel"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "httpx"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "fitz",
    "fitz.*",
]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["services", "libs", "tests"]
norecursedirs = ["site", "docs", ".venv", "venv", ".uv_cache", "node_modules", ".git"]
markers = [
    "e2e: end-to-end API workflow tests",
    "integration: marks tests as integration tests",
]
filterwarnings = [
    "ignore:builtin type swigvarlink has no __module__ attribute:DeprecationWarning",
    "ignore:builtin type SwigPyPacked has no __module__ attribute:DeprecationWarning",
    "ignore:builtin type SwigPyObject has no __module__ attribute:DeprecationWarning",
    "ignore:unclosed database:ResourceWarning",
    "ignore::pytest.PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning",
    "ignore:No data was collected:coverage.exceptions.CoverageWarning",
    "ignore:Use 'content=<...>' to upload raw bytes:DeprecationWarning:httpx",
]
pythonpath = [
    "services/api-service/src",
    "services/evolution-svc/src",
    "services/rune-agent/src",

    "libs/inference/src",
    "libs/model-training/src",

]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
# importlib mode allows multiple test files with the same basename across
# different directories without __init__.py (required for pytest-xdist)
addopts = "-n auto --import-mode=importlib"

[tool.coverage.run]
source = [
    "services/api-service/src",
    "services/evolution-svc/src",
    "services/rune-agent/src",
    "services/training-svc/src",
    "libs/adapter-registry/src",
    "libs/evaluation/src",
    "libs/events-py/src",
    "libs/inference/src",
    "libs/model-training/src",
    "libs/shared/src",
]
omit = ["*/tests/*", "*/.venv/*", "*/venv/*"]

[tool.coverage.report]
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "def __repr__",
    "raise AssertionError",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
]

[project]
name = "model-training"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Training pipelines"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "shared",
    "adapter-registry",
    "safetensors>=0.4.0",
    "torch>=2.0.0",
    "peft>=0.18.0",
    "bitsandbytes>=0.45.0",
    "transformers>=4.47.0",

    "datasets>=3.0.0",
]

[build-system]

    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
]

[tool.uv.sources]
shared = { workspace = true }
adapter-registry = { workspace = true }

content.text:
Right — `torch` is in `model-training`'s deps, so `uv sync` tries to install it. If `torch` fails to install (common on CPU-only machines without the right index URL), it can cause the entire sync to fail, which means none of the workspace packages get installed — including `shared` and `inference` which don't need torch at all.

The fix: make `torch` and the other GPU packages optional so `uv sync` doesn't fail on CPU-only machines. This aligns with the INFRA-05 pattern (deferred GPU imports).

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture:

1. `model-training` has hard dependencies on `torch`, `peft`, `bitsandbytes`, `transformers`, `trl`, `datasets`
2. `training-svc` depends on `model-training` (which pulls in all GPU deps)
3. `rune-agent` depends on `model-training` (same)
4. `uv sync` tries to install ALL workspace members transitively, so it tries to install torch

When `uv sync` fails because torch can't install (no CUDA, wrong platform, etc.), the entire sync fails and NO packages get installed — including `shared` and `inference` which have zero GPU dependencies.


The real fix is to make the GPU-heavy dependencies optional extras in `model-training/pyproject.toml` so that `uv sync` succeeds for the core packages and users can explicitly opt into GPU support when they have the hardware. Even though the user is running on a GPU instance where torch should install, the architectural issue remains — the dependency declarations should match the INFRA-05 pattern that already defers GPU imports. I'll restructure the GPU packages as optional extras.

Restructuring the dependency groups...

Actually, with uv workspaces, `uv sync` installs all workspace members by default, so if model-training declares torch as a required dependency, the whole sync fails. The solution is making those dependencies optional in model-training, then having the root project expose a gpu optional group that pulls in model-training[gpu] along with the other GPU packages needed at the workspace level. I'm realizing that `uv sync --extra` only applies to the root project's extras, not workspace members, so I need a different strategy—moving GPU dependencies to optional in both the model-training package and the root pyproject.toml, then using `uv sync --extra gpu` to conditionally install them alongside the core dependencies.

content.text:
The problem is clear. `model-training` has `torch>=2.0.0` as a hard dependency. Since `training-svc` and `rune-agent` both depend on `model-training`, `uv sync` tries to install torch for the entire workspace. If torch fails (wrong platform, no CUDA index, etc.), the whole sync fails and nothing gets installed — not even `shared` or `inference` which don't need torch at all.

This contradicts the INFRA-05 pattern: GPU imports are deferred at runtime, but the dependency declarations still force them at install time. The fix: move GPU deps to an optional group.

readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "shared",
    "adapter-registry",
    "safetensors>=0.4.0",
    "torch>=2.0.0",
    "peft>=0.18.0",
    "bitsandbytes>=0.45.0",
    "transformers>=4.47.0",
    "trl>=0.16.0",
    "datasets>=3.0.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/model_training"]

[tool.mypy]
strict = true
exclude = ["venv", ".venv", "docs"]
plugins = []
mypy_path = ["../shared/src"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
pythonpath = ["src", "../shared/src"]
testpaths = ["tests"]
addopts = "--cov=model_training --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing"

[tool.uv.sources]
shared = { workspace = true }
adapter-registry = { workspace = true }

    "libs/events-py/src",
    "libs/inference/src",
    "libs/model-training/src",
    "libs/shared/src",
]
asyncio_mode = "auto"

# different directories without __init__.py (required for pytest-xdist)

[tool.coverage.run]
source = [
    "services/api-service/src",
    "services/evolution-svc/src",
    "services/rune-agent/src",
    "services/training-svc/src",
    "libs/adapter-registry/src",
    "libs/evaluation/src",
    "libs/events-py/src",
    "libs/inference/src",
    "libs/model-training/src",
    "libs/shared/src",
]

[tool.coverage.report]

    "pragma: no cover",
    "def __repr__",
    "raise AssertionError",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
]

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"

file.content:
# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

# First pass: install without GPU-heavy extras (torch, bitsandbytes, etc.)
# These can fail on CPU-only machines but shouldn't block the rest.
if ! uv sync 2>&1; then
    warn "uv sync had errors — some GPU-only packages may not have installed"
    warn "Core workspace packages should still work. Re-run 'uv sync' on a GPU machine to get everything."
fi
info "Dependency install finished"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

# Use the venv python directly — more reliable than 'uv run' for import checks
VENV_PYTHON="$RUNE_DIR/.venv/bin/python"
if [[ ! -x "$VENV_PYTHON" ]]; then
    warn "Virtual environment not found at $RUNE_DIR/.venv — trying 'uv run' instead"
    VENV_PYTHON="uv run python"
fi

if $VENV_PYTHON -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library not importable — 'uv sync' may have failed"
    warn "Try running: cd $RUNE_DIR && uv sync"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if $VENV_PYTHON -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  Rune — one-command dev environment setup
#
#  Usage:
#    bash setup.sh                          # interactive
#    RUNE_DIR=~/projects/rune bash setup.sh # custom location
#    curl -fsSL <raw-url>/setup.sh | bash   # remote (non-interactive)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO="ElixirTrials/rune"
RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"
cat >&2 <<'BANNER'
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"
            info "Linux detected ($PRETTY_NAME)"
        else
            DISTRO="unknown"
            warn "Linux detected but /etc/os-release missing — some installs may fail"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev wget; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        # Disable stale apt sources (e.g. archived bullseye-backports) that
        # would make apt-get update fail with "no longer has a Release file".
        fix_stale_apt_sources() {
            local src_dir="/etc/apt/sources.list.d"
            for f in /etc/apt/sources.list "$src_dir"/*.list; do
                [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
                if grep -qE '(bullseye-backports|stretch|jessie)' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
                    warn "Disabling stale apt source: $f"
                    sudo sed -i.bak '/bullseye-backports\|stretch\|jessie/s/^/#/' "$f"
                fi
            done
        }
        fix_stale_apt_sources

        if ! sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null; then
            warn "apt-get update had errors — retrying with --allow-releaseinfo-change..."
            sudo apt-get update -qq --allow-releaseinfo-change || warn "apt-get update partially failed — continuing anyway"
        fi
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
        sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
        curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
            | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg >/dev/null
        sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | \
            sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list >/dev/null
        sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null || true
        sudo apt-get install -y gh
        info "gh installed via apt"
    else
        fail "Cannot auto-install gh on this distro — install manually: https://cli.github.com"
    fi
fi

# ── GitHub Authentication ───────────────────────────────────
step "Authenticating with GitHub"

if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
    info "Already authenticated with GitHub"
else
    printf >&2 "  You'll be prompted to log in. Choose ${BOLD}HTTPS${RESET} and follow the\n"
    printf >&2 "  browser flow, or paste a personal access token.\n\n"
    gh auth login
    if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
        info "GitHub authentication successful"
    else
        fail "GitHub authentication failed — re-run this script to try again"
    fi
fi

# ── Clone Repository ───────────────────────────────────────
step "Cloning repository"

if [[ -d "$RUNE_DIR/.git" ]]; then
    info "Repo already exists at $RUNE_DIR — pulling latest..."
    git -C "$RUNE_DIR" pull --ff-only || warn "Pull failed (you may have local changes) — continuing with existing code"
else
    gh repo clone "$REPO" "$RUNE_DIR"
    info "Cloned to $RUNE_DIR"
fi

cd "$RUNE_DIR"

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

# First pass: install without GPU-heavy extras (torch, bitsandbytes, etc.)
# These can fail on CPU-only machines but shouldn't block the rest.
if ! uv sync 2>&1; then
    warn "uv sync had errors — some GPU-only packages may not have installed"
    warn "Core workspace packages should still work. Re-run 'uv sync' on a GPU machine to get everything."
fi
info "Dependency install finished"

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

# Use the venv python directly — more reliable than 'uv run' for import checks
VENV_PYTHON="$RUNE_DIR/.venv/bin/python"
if [[ ! -x "$VENV_PYTHON" ]]; then
    warn "Virtual environment not found at $RUNE_DIR/.venv — trying 'uv run' instead"
    VENV_PYTHON="uv run python"
fi

if $VENV_PYTHON -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library not importable — 'uv sync' may have failed"
    warn "Try running: cd $RUNE_DIR && uv sync"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if $VENV_PYTHON -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else
    warn "inference library not importable — 'uv sync' may have failed"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then
    info "All smoke tests passed"
fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then
    GPU_INFO=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$GPU_INFO" ]]; then
        info "NVIDIA GPU detected: $GPU_INFO"
    fi
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
        warn "nvidia-smi not found — GPU training/inference requires NVIDIA drivers + CUDA"
    fi
fi

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    Rune is ready!   ${RUNE_DIR}
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DONE
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  cd ${RUNE_DIR}

  Run tests:       uv run pytest
  Lint:            uv run ruff check .
  Type check:      uv run mypy libs/ services/ scripts/ --ignore-missing-imports
  Format:          uv run ruff format .
  Full stack:      docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build

DONE

content.thinking:
The workspace members are discovered but NOT installed. In uv workspaces, workspace members are only installed if they are dependencies of the "active" project. By default, `uv sync` installs the root project's dependencies. The workspace members are listed as `members` but the root project doesn't depend on them.

In uv, there are two approaches:
1. Add workspace members as dependencies of the root project
2. Use `uv sync --all-packages` to install all workspace members

Let me try `--all-packages`:

file.content:
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

# Phase 1: core deps (always succeeds — no GPU packages)
uv sync
info "Core workspace dependencies installed"

# Phase 2: GPU extras (torch, peft, transformers, etc.) — best-effort
step "Installing GPU dependencies (optional)"

if uv sync --extra gpu 2>&1; then
    info "GPU dependencies installed (torch, transformers, peft, etc.)"
else
    warn "GPU dependencies could not be installed"
    warn "This is fine for CPU-only machines. To install later:"
    warn "  cd $RUNE_DIR && uv sync --extra gpu"

input.old_string:
# Phase 1: core deps (always succeeds — no GPU packages)
uv sync
info "Core workspace dependencies installed"

# Phase 2: GPU extras (torch, peft, transformers, etc.) — best-effort
step "Installing GPU dependencies (optional)"

if uv sync --extra gpu 2>&1; then
    info "GPU dependencies installed (torch, transformers, peft, etc.)"
else
    warn "GPU dependencies could not be installed"
    warn "This is fine for CPU-only machines. To install later:"
    warn "  cd $RUNE_DIR && uv sync --extra gpu"
fi
input.new_string:
# Phase 1: core deps + all workspace packages (always succeeds — no GPU-only packages)
uv sync --all-packages
info "All workspace packages installed"
