toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#  Rune — one-command dev environment setup
#
#  Usage:
#    bash setup.sh                          # interactive
#    RUNE_DIR=~/projects/rune bash setup.sh # custom location
#    curl -fsSL <raw-url>/setup.sh | bash   # remote (non-interactive)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

# ── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
REPO="ElixirTrials/rune"
RUNE_DIR="${RUNE_DIR:-$HOME/rune}"
REQUIRED_PYTHON="3.12"

# ── Colors (respect NO_COLOR: https://no-color.org) ─────────
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]] && [[ -t 2 ]]; then
    BOLD='\033[1m'    RESET='\033[0m'
    RED='\033[0;31m'  GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
else
    BOLD='' RESET='' RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' CYAN=''
fi

# ── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
info()  { printf "${GREEN}[OK]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
warn()  { printf "${YELLOW}[!!]${RESET}  %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
fail()  { printf "${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} %s\n" "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
step()  { printf "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}▸ %s${RESET}\n" "$*" >&2; }

# ── Banner ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n${BOLD}${CYAN}"
cat >&2 <<'BANNER'
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               R U N E                       │
  │   AI agent + LoRA training + swarm infra    │
  │   github.com/ElixirTrials/rune              │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
BANNER
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
printf >&2 "  This script will install system deps, authenticate with\n"
printf >&2 "  GitHub, clone the repo, and set up the Python environment.\n\n"
printf >&2 "  Install location: ${BOLD}%s${RESET}\n\n" "$RUNE_DIR"

# Confirm if running interactively
if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
    read -rp "  Continue? [Y/n] " answer
    case "${answer:-Y}" in
        [Yy]*|"") ;;
        *) echo "Aborted." >&2; exit 0 ;;
    esac
fi

# ── OS Detection ────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Detecting operating system"

OS="$(uname -s)"
DISTRO=""

case "$OS" in
    Darwin)
        info "macOS detected"
        ;;
    Linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            # shellcheck source=/dev/null
            . /etc/os-release
            DISTRO="${ID:-unknown}"
            info "Linux detected ($PRETTY_NAME)"
        else
            DISTRO="unknown"
            warn "Linux detected but /etc/os-release missing — some installs may fail"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        fail "Unsupported OS: $OS (this script supports macOS and Linux)"
        ;;
esac

# ── Helper: check if a command exists ───────────────────────
has() { command -v "$1" &>/dev/null; }

# ── System Packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing system packages"

if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    if ! has brew; then
        warn "Homebrew not found — installing..."
        /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
        if [[ -f /opt/homebrew/bin/brew ]]; then
            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
        fi
        info "Homebrew installed"
    else
        info "Homebrew already installed"
    fi

    for pkg in git curl; do
        if has "$pkg"; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            brew install "$pkg"
            info "$pkg installed"
        fi
    done

elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
    NEEDED=()
    for pkg in git curl build-essential libssl-dev wget; do
        if dpkg -s "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
            info "$pkg already installed"
        else
            NEEDED+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done
    if [[ ${#NEEDED[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        # Disable stale apt sources (e.g. archived bullseye-backports) that
        # would make apt-get update fail with "no longer has a Release file".
        fix_stale_apt_sources() {
            local src_dir="/etc/apt/sources.list.d"
            for f in /etc/apt/sources.list "$src_dir"/*.list; do
                [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
                if grep -qE '(bullseye-backports|stretch|jessie)' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
                    warn "Disabling stale apt source: $f"
                    sudo sed -i.bak '/bullseye-backports\|stretch\|jessie/s/^/#/' "$f"
                fi
            done
        }
        fix_stale_apt_sources

        if ! sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null; then
            warn "apt-get update had errors — retrying with --allow-releaseinfo-change..."
            sudo apt-get update -qq --allow-releaseinfo-change || warn "apt-get update partially failed — continuing anyway"
        fi
        sudo apt-get install -y "${NEEDED[@]}"
        info "Installed: ${NEEDED[*]}"
    fi
else
    warn "Skipping system package install — please ensure git and curl are available"
fi

# ── GitHub CLI ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Setting up GitHub CLI"

if has gh; then
    info "gh already installed ($(gh --version | head -1))"
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        brew install gh
        info "gh installed via Homebrew"

    elif [[ "$DISTRO" == "ubuntu" || "$DISTRO" == "debian" ]]; then
        sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
        curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
            | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg >/dev/null
        sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
        echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | \
            sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list >/dev/null
        sudo apt-get update -qq 2>/dev/null || true
        sudo apt-get install -y gh
        info "gh installed via apt"
    else
        fail "Cannot auto-install gh on this distro — install manually: https://cli.github.com"
    fi
fi

# ── GitHub Authentication ───────────────────────────────────
step "Authenticating with GitHub"

if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
    info "Already authenticated with GitHub"
else
    printf >&2 "  You'll be prompted to log in. Choose ${BOLD}HTTPS${RESET} and follow the\n"
    printf >&2 "  browser flow, or paste a personal access token.\n\n"
    gh auth login
    if gh auth status &>/dev/null; then
        info "GitHub authentication successful"
    else
        fail "GitHub authentication failed — re-run this script to try again"
    fi
fi

# ── Clone Repository ───────────────────────────────────────
step "Cloning repository"

if [[ -d "$RUNE_DIR/.git" ]]; then
    info "Repo already exists at $RUNE_DIR — pulling latest..."
    git -C "$RUNE_DIR" pull --ff-only || warn "Pull failed (you may have local changes) — continuing with existing code"
else
    gh repo clone "$REPO" "$RUNE_DIR"
    info "Cloned to $RUNE_DIR"
fi

cd "$RUNE_DIR"

# ── Install uv ─────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Installing uv (Python package manager)"

if has uv; then
    info "uv already installed ($(uv --version))"
else
    curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    # Make uv available in this session
    export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
    if has uv; then
        info "uv installed ($(uv --version))"
    else
        fail "uv install succeeded but binary not found on PATH"
    fi
fi

# ── Python + Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────
step "Installing Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON and project dependencies"

uv python install "$REQUIRED_PYTHON"
info "Python $REQUIRED_PYTHON ready"

# Phase 1: core deps (always succeeds — no GPU packages)
uv sync
info "Core workspace dependencies installed"

# Phase 2: GPU extras (torch, peft, transformers, etc.) — best-effort
step "Installing GPU dependencies (optional)"

if uv sync --extra gpu 2>&1; then
    info "GPU dependencies installed (torch, transformers, peft, etc.)"
else
    warn "GPU dependencies could not be installed"
    warn "This is fine for CPU-only machines. To install later:"
    warn "  cd $RUNE_DIR && uv sync --extra gpu"
fi

# ── Smoke Test ──────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Running smoke tests"

SMOKE_PASS=true

# Use the venv python directly — more reliable than 'uv run' for import checks
VENV_PYTHON="$RUNE_DIR/.venv/bin/python"
if [[ ! -x "$VENV_PYTHON" ]]; then
    warn "Virtual environment not found at $RUNE_DIR/.venv — trying 'uv run' instead"
    VENV_PYTHON="uv run python"
fi

if $VENV_PYTHON -c "from shared.rune_models import TaskStatus; print('  shared lib:', TaskStatus.PENDING.value)" 2>&1; then
    info "shared library OK"
else
    warn "shared library not importable — 'uv sync' may have failed"
    warn "Try running: cd $RUNE_DIR && uv sync"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if $VENV_PYTHON -c "from inference.provider import InferenceProvider; print('  inference provider ABC loaded')" 2>&1; then
    info "inference library OK"
else
    warn "inference library not importable — 'uv sync' may have failed"
    SMOKE_PASS=false
fi

if [[ "$SMOKE_PASS" == true ]]; then
    info "All smoke tests passed"
fi

# ── Advisory Checks ────────────────────────────────────────
step "Checking optional tools"

if has docker; then
    info "Docker found ($(docker --version | cut -d' ' -f3 | tr -d ','))"
else
    warn "Docker not found — install it to use the full stack (infra/docker-compose.yml)"
fi

if has nvidia-smi; then
    GPU_INFO=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total --format=csv,noheader 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$GPU_INFO" ]]; then
        info "NVIDIA GPU detected: $GPU_INFO"
    fi
else
    if [[ "$OS" == "Linux" ]]; then
        warn "nvidia-smi not found — GPU training/inference requires NVIDIA drivers + CUDA"
    fi
fi

# ── Done ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
printf >&2 "\n"
printf >&2 "${BOLD}${GREEN}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    Rune is ready!   ${RUNE_DIR}
  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DONE
printf >&2 "${RESET}"
cat >&2 <<DONE
  cd ${RUNE_DIR}

  Run tests:       uv run pytest
  Lint:            uv run ruff check .
  Type check:      uv run mypy libs/ services/ scripts/ --ignore-missing-imports
  Format:          uv run ruff format .
  Full stack:      docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build

DONE

content.text:
Here's a summary of the two fixes:

**1. Smoke test failures** — `uv sync` wasn't using `--all-packages`, so workspace members (shared, inference, etc.) were never installed. Fixed by adding `--all-packages` to all `uv sync` calls in `setup.sh`.

**2. GPU dep architecture** — `model-training` had `torch`, `peft`, `transformers`, etc. as hard dependencies. If any failed to install, the entire `uv sync` would fail and nothing got installed. Fixed by:
- Moving GPU-only deps (`bitsandbytes`, `trl`, `datasets`) to `[project.optional-dependencies] gpu` in `model-training/pyproject.toml`
- The user moved `torch`, `safetensors`, `peft`, `transformers`, `huggingface-hub` to root deps (they install fine on CPU)
- Setup script now does: `uv sync --all-packages` (always works), then `uv sync --all-packages --extra gpu` (best-effort)

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## COMPREHENSIVE HYPERNETWORK IMPLEMENTATION REPORT

Based on my thorough exploration of the rune monorepo, here are ALL the critical details about the hypernetwork implementation:

### 1. **HYPERNETWORK FILE LOCATION**
- **Primary file:** `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/src/model_training/hypernetwork.py` (404 lines)

### 2. **ARCHITECTURE PATTERN: `_LazyHypernetworkProxy` + `_build_hypernetwork_class`**

The implementation uses a deferred class-building pattern to satisfy INFRA-05 (CPU-only importability):

**Pattern Flow:**
1. `DocToLoraHypernetwork` is a module-level `_LazyHypernetworkProxy` instance (line 231)
2. When first instantiated, `_LazyHypernetworkProxy.__call__()` triggers `_build_hypernetwork_class()` (line 222)
3. `_build_hypernetwork_class()` imports torch/nn only then (line 41-42) and returns a real `nn.Module` subclass
4. All subsequent instantiations reuse the cached class via `_real_class`

This keeps the module importable without torch at module level.

### 3. **DocToLoraHypernetwork CLASS SIGNATURE**

Located within `_build_hypernetwork_class()` (lines 44-199), returns `_DocToLoraHypernetwork(nn.Module)`.

**`__init__` signature (lines 75-87):**
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    input_dim: int,
    num_latents: int = 32,
    latent_dim: int = 256,
    depth: int = 4,
    heads: int = 8,
    rank: int = 8,
    target_modules: Sequence[str] = ("q_proj", "v_proj"),
    num_layers: int = 28,
    hidden_dim: int = 4096,
    module_dims: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] | None = None,
) -> None:
```

**All attributes initialized (lines 88-103):**
- `self.rank: int = rank` (default: 8)
- `self.hidden_dim: int = hidden_dim` (default: 4096)
- `self.target_modules: list[str]` (default: ["q_proj", "v_proj"])
- `self.num_layers: int = num_layers` (default: 28)
- `self.num_latents: int = num_latents` (default: 32)
- `self.latent_dim: int = latent_dim` (default: 256)
- `self.module_dims: dict[str, tuple[int, int]]` — maps module name to (in_features, out_features)

### 4. **MODULE ATTRIBUTES (nn.Module submodules)**

**Exact initialization (lines 106-129):**

1. **`self.token_embedding`** (line 106):
   ```python
   nn.Embedding(input_dim, latent_dim)
   ```
   - Hypernetwork's own lightweight embedding (NOT the 7B model's embedding)
   - Input: vocab indices, output: latent_dim embeddings

2. **`self.latents`** (line 109):
   ```python
   nn.Parameter(torch.randn(num_latents, latent_dim))
   ```
   - Learnable parameter tensor
   - **FIXED SHAPE: (32, 256)** — tests confirm this is always (num_latents=32, latent_dim=256) by design
   - Unexpanded shape; expanded in forward() per batch

3. **`self.cross_attend`** (lines 112-114):
   ```python
   nn.MultiheadAttention(latent_dim, heads, batch_first=True)
   ```
   - Latents (Q) cross-attend over token embeddings (K, V)
   - Input: latent_dim=256, heads=8

4. **`self.self_attend`** (lines 117-120):
   ```python
   encoder_layer = nn.TransformerEncoderLayer(latent_dim, heads, batch_first=True)
   nn.TransformerEncoder(encoder_layer, num_layers=depth)
   ```

### 13. **TEST COVERAGE**

**Files:** `/libs/model-training/tests/test_hypernetwork.py`

Key tests:

- **test_hypernetwork_latents_shape** — verifies latents always (32, 256)

- **test_hypernetwork_forward_lora_shapes** — validates lora_A (rank, hidden), lora_B (hidden, rank)

### 14. **KEY DIMENSIONS SUMMARY**

| Parameter | Default | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| input_dim | (required) | Vocab size for hypernetwork's embedding |
| num_latents | 32 | FIXED for tests |
| latent_dim | 256 | FIXED for tests |
| depth | 4 | Self-attention stack layers |
| heads | 8 | Attention heads |
| rank | 8 | LoRA rank for generated adapters |
| target_modules | ["q_proj", "v_proj"] | Default PEFT targets |

| hidden_dim | 4096 | Target model hidden dim |
| module_dims | None | Override per-module dims (e.g., GQA KV cache) |

### 15. **CRITICAL PEFT STATE_DICT KEY FORMAT**

All generated weights use this exact pattern:
```
base_model.model.model.layers.{layer_idx}.self_attn.{module_name}.lora_{A|B}.weight
```

Examples:
- `base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.weight`
- `base_model.model.model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_B.weight`
- `base_model.model.model.layers.27.self_attn.v_proj.lora_A.weight`

### 16. **INFRA-05 COMPLIANCE**

All GPU imports are deferred inside function/method bodies:
- `hypernetwork.py`: torch imports only in `_build_hypernetwork_class()`, `load_pretrained()`, `trajectory_to_tokens()`, `generate_adapter()`, `save_hypernetwork_adapter()`
- `trainer.py`: All imports inside `train_qlora()`, `train_and_register()`
- `trajectory.py`: No GPU imports at all
- `config.py`: No GPU imports at all
- `peft_utils.py`: peft imports only inside functions

This keeps all modules importable in CPU-only CI environments.

---

**Summary:** The hypernetwork implementation is a Perceiver-based instant LoRA adapter generator that produces rank-8 LoRA weights in <1s without loading the 7B base model, using learned latents attending over token embeddings extracted from trajectory text. It generates PEFT-compatible adapters with exact state_dict keys for Qwen2.5-7B's 28 transformer layers.

```python
def get_best_device() -> str:
    """Return: 'cuda' > 'mps' > 'cpu'"""
    # Deferred import pattern (INFRA-05)
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        return "cuda"

        return "mps"
    return "cpu"
```

**Constants:**

```python
VRAM_PER_LORA_MB: int = 100        # Per adapter
MIN_TRAINING_VRAM_MB: int = 4096   # Minimum for training job
```

**HardwareBudget Computation:**

- `training_slots = 1 if free_vram >= MIN_TRAINING_VRAM_MB else 0`

### 7. HYPERNETWORK ARCHITECTURE (`libs/model-training/src/model_training/hypernetwork.py`)

**DocToLoraHypernetwork (Custom Implementation):**

Default Parameters:
```python
DEFAULT_VOCAB_SIZE: int = 32000

class DocToLoraHypernetwork:
    input_dim: int                          # Vocab size for token embedding
    num_latents: int = 32                   # Learnable latent vectors
    latent_dim: int = 256                   # Latent dimensionality
    depth: int = 4                          # Self-attention layers
    heads: int = 8                          # Attention heads
    rank: int = 8                           # LoRA rank for generated adapters
    target_modules: tuple = ("q_proj", "v_proj")
    num_layers: int = 28                    # Transformer layers in base model
    hidden_dim: int = 4096                  # Default hidden dimension
    module_dims: dict | None                # Per-module dims for GQA support
```

**Architecture:**
1. Token embedding (hypernetwork's own, NOT base model's)
2. Learnable latent array: `(num_latents, latent_dim)`
3. Cross-attention: latents attend over token embeddings
4. Self-attention stack on latents
5. Linear head: project to all LoRA weight parameters

**Key Functions:**
- `load_pretrained(checkpoint_path, device)` - Load from checkpoint
- `trajectory_to_tokens(text, vocab_size, max_length)` - Hash-based tokenization
- `generate_adapter(hypernetwork, trajectory_text, output_dir, base_model_id)`
- `save_hypernetwork_adapter(weights, output_dir, base_model_id, rank, target_modules)`

**Note:** This is a separate, custom implementation from Sakana's `ctx-to-lora`. The custom one uses simple hash-based tokenization, while Sakana's uses more sophisticated perceiver architecture.

5. **Step 5:** Full Iteration Loop: DocToLoraHypernetwork (custom, NOT Sakana) via rune_runner

**`scripts/e2e_smoke.py`** (Smoke Test - CPU Only)
- NO GPU required
- Tests: Adapter Registry, Inference Provider ABC, Agent nodes, QLoRA config, Hypernetwork forward pass, Evaluation metrics, Training Service HTTP endpoints

- `qwen_4b_d2l`: `qwen_4b_d2l/checkpoint-20000/pytorch_model.bin`

**Flash Attention Patching:**
- Sakana code uses flash_attention_2 and fails on CPU/MPS
- `_patch_flash_attention()` replaces flash classes with eager attention
- Patches: `Idefics2PerceiverAttention`, `Idefics2PerceiverResampler`, `Idefics2Perceiver`, `Idefics2PerceiverConfig`

**Activation Extraction:**
- Loads tokenizer from base model
- Loads base model in float32 for activation extraction
- Captures hidden states at specified layer indices
- Cleans up model memory after extraction

### 12. RUNE ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

**Three Interacting Components:**

1. **Recursive Code Generation Loop**
   - Bootstrap → Generate → Execute → Reflect
   - Trajectory → Hypernetwork → Adapter
   - Compose adapter into library

2. **Adapter Hierarchy**
   - Project root adapter
   - Domain adapters (async, FastAPI, etc.)
   - Task-specific adapters
   - Evolution Operator manages lifecycle

3. **Serving Architecture**
   - vLLM server with dynamic LoRA adapter loading
   - Adapter registry (SQLite metadata)
   - S-LoRA unified paging for concurrent adapter serving

**Key Innovation:** LoRA weight space as persistent episodic memory across sessions.

```python
    """Return: 'cuda' > 'mps' > 'cpu'"""
    # Deferred import pattern (INFRA-05)
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        return "cuda"
    if torch.backends.mps.is_available():
        return "mps"
    return "cpu"
```

**Constants:**
```python
VRAM_PER_LORA_MB: int = 100        # Per adapter
MIN_TRAINING_VRAM_MB: int = 4096   # Minimum for training job
```

**HardwareBudget Computation:**
- `max_concurrent_loras = max(1, free_vram // VRAM_PER_LORA_MB)`
- `training_slots = 1 if free_vram >= MIN_TRAINING_VRAM_MB else 0`

---

### 7. HYPERNETWORK ARCHITECTURE (`libs/model-training/src/model_training/hypernetwork.py`)

**DocToLoraHypernetwork (Custom Implementation):**

Default Parameters:
```python
DEFAULT_VOCAB_SIZE: int = 32000

class DocToLoraHypernetwork:
    input_dim: int                          # Vocab size for token embedding
    num_latents: int = 32                   # Learnable latent vectors
    latent_dim: int = 256                   # Latent dimensionality
    depth: int = 4                          # Self-attention layers
    heads: int = 8                          # Attention heads
    rank: int = 8                           # LoRA rank for generated adapters
    target_modules: tuple = ("q_proj", "v_proj")
    num_layers: int = 28                    # Transformer layers in base model
    hidden_dim: int = 4096                  # Default hidden dimension
    module_dims: dict | None                # Per-module dims for GQA support
```

**Architecture:**
1. Token embedding (hypernetwork's own, NOT base model's)
2. Learnable latent array: `(num_latents, latent_dim)`
3. Cross-attention: latents attend over token embeddings
4. Self-attention stack on latents
5. Linear head: project to all LoRA weight parameters

**Key Functions:**
- `load_pretrained(checkpoint_path, device)` - Load from checkpoint
- `trajectory_to_tokens(text, vocab_size, max_length)` - Hash-based tokenization
- `generate_adapter(hypernetwork, trajectory_text, output_dir, base_model_id)`
- `save_hypernetwork_adapter(weights, output_dir, base_model_id, rank, target_modules)`

**Note:** This is a separate, custom implementation from Sakana's `ctx-to-lora`. The custom one uses simple hash-based tokenization, while Sakana's uses more sophisticated perceiver architecture.

### 12. RUNE ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

**Three Interacting Components:**

1. **Recursive Code Generation Loop**
   - Bootstrap → Generate → Execute → Reflect
   - Trajectory → Hypernetwork → Adapter
   - Compose adapter into library

2. **Adapter Hierarchy**
   - Project root adapter
   - Domain adapters (async, FastAPI, etc.)
   - Task-specific adapters
   - Evolution Operator manages lifecycle

3. **Serving Architecture**
   - vLLM server with dynamic LoRA adapter loading
   - Adapter registry (SQLite metadata)
   - S-LoRA unified paging for concurrent adapter serving

**Key Innovation:** LoRA weight space as persistent episodic memory across sessions.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Rune Monorepo Training Service & Test Patterns

Based on my thorough exploration of the rune monorepo, here is a complete analysis of the training service architecture, test patterns, mock strategies, and reusable code:

---

### 1. TRAINING SERVICE STRUCTURE

#### Main Entry Point: `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/services/training-svc/src/training_svc/routers/training.py`

**Key Functions:**

- `_run_hypernetwork_job(job_id, adapter_id, trajectory_id, task_type)`: Background worker that:
  - Takes 4 arguments (as documented in memory)
  - Loads trajectory via `load_trajectory(trajectory_id)`
  - Formats messages via `format_for_sft(trajectory)` 
  - Tokenizes text using `AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_id)`
  - Instantiates `DocToLoraHypernetwork(input_dim=tokenizer.vocab_size)`
  - Loads pretrained weights via `torch.load(hypernetwork_weights_path, map_location="cpu")`
  - Runs forward pass: `weights = hypernetwork(token_ids)` (with `torch.no_grad()`)
  - Saves adapter via `save_hypernetwork_adapter(weights, adapter_dir, base_model_id)`
  - Registers in `AdapterRegistry` with metadata (source="hypernetwork", rank=8, session_id=trajectory_id)
  - Updates `JOB_STORE[job_id]` status to "completed" or "failed"

- `train_hypernetwork(request, background_tasks)`: FastAPI endpoint that:
  - Validates `trajectory_ids` is non-empty (raises 422 if empty)
  - Assigns `adapter_id` at request time (not at job completion)
  - Queues `_run_hypernetwork_job` as background task
  - Returns immediately with `{"job_id": ..., "status": "queued"}`

**Environment Variables Used:**
```python
RUNE_BASE_MODEL = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct" (default)
RUNE_HYPERNETWORK_WEIGHTS_PATH = Path.home() / ".rune" / "hypernetwork.pt" (default)
RUNE_ADAPTER_DIR = optional, defaults to ~/.rune/adapters/
RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR = optional, set by tests via monkeypatch
```

**Thread Safety Pattern:**
- Uses `_JOB_STORE_LOCK = threading.Lock()` from `training_svc.jobs`
- All `JOB_STORE[job_id]` access wrapped with `with _JOB_STORE_LOCK:`

```python
def _make_factory(cls: type[T], defaults: dict[str, Any]) -> Callable[..., T]:
    """Return a factory callable that creates *cls* instances with merged defaults."""
    def _factory(**kwargs: Any) -> T:
        return cls(**{**defaults, **kwargs})
    return _factory
```

```python
    app.dependency_overrides.clear()
```

### 8. PYTEST MARKERS & XFAIL PATTERNS

**skipif markers:**

- `@pytest.mark.xfail(reason="Full GPU mocking chain is fragile in CPU CI", strict=False)` in test_trainer.py

### 9. KEY HYPERNETWORK IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

#### Lazy Proxy Pattern (`hypernetwork.py` lines 210-231):
```python
class _LazyHypernetworkProxy:
    """Lazy proxy for DocToLoraHypernetwork.
    
    Acts as a callable that creates a real nn.Module instance on first call.
    The class is rebuilt (and cached) on first instantiation so that torch is
    only imported when actually needed (INFRA-05 pattern).
    """
    _real_class: type | None = None
    
    def __call__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
        if self._real_class is None:
            self.__class__._real_class = _build_hypernetwork_class()
        return self._real_class(*args, **kwargs)
    
    def __instancecheck__(self, instance: object) -> bool:
        if self._real_class is None:
            return False
        return isinstance(instance, self._real_class)

DocToLoraHypernetwork: _LazyHypernetworkProxy = _LazyHypernetworkProxy()
```

#### Constructor Args:
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    input_dim: int,  # vocab_size from tokenizer
    num_latents: int = 32,
    latent_dim: int = 256,
    depth: int = 4,
    heads: int = 8,
    rank: int = 8,  # Fixed to 8 for hypernetwork
    target_modules: Sequence[str] = ("q_proj", "v_proj"),
    num_layers: int = 28,  # Target model (Qwen2.5-7B)
    hidden_dim: int = 4096,  # Target model hidden dim
    module_dims: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] | None = None,  # For GQA
) -> None:
```

#### forward() Signature:
```python
def forward(self, token_ids: torch.Tensor) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    """Generate PEFT-compatible LoRA adapter weights from token IDs.
    
    Args:
        token_ids: Integer token ID tensor of shape (batch, seq_len).
    
    Returns:
        Dict mapping PEFT state_dict keys to weight tensors.
        Keys follow:
          base_model.model.model.layers.{i}.self_attn.{module}.lora_{A|B}.weight
        Returns first batch element (one adapter per trajectory).
    """
```

### 11. UNUSED/NOT-YET-IMPLEMENTED CODE

**No test_hypernetwork_qwen3.py or test_d2l_data.py exists** (as noted in the exploration request).

---

### 12. CRUCIAL DESIGN DECISIONS

1. **Hypernetwork rank is fixed to 8** - not configurable per adapter
2. **adapter_id is assigned at request time**, not at job completion (line 276 in training.py)
3. **No modules_to_save in LoraConfig** - vLLM rejects this (per Phase 21-01 decision)
4. **embed_tokens and lm_head excluded** from target_modules to prevent vLLM conflicts
5. **Token IDs come from base model's tokenizer**, not custom tokenization - hypernetwork learns its own embedding
6. **JOB_STORE is in-memory dict**, not database - status only tracked in RAM during job execution
7. **All GPU imports deferred to function bodies** - module-level imports are stdlib only
8. **Adapter files stored with sha256 hash** of safetensors file for integrity tracking

content.text:
Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information about the training service and test patterns. Let me now compile a detailed report.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Rune Monorepo Training Service & Test Patterns

Based on my thorough exploration of the rune monorepo, here is a complete analysis of the training service architecture, test patterns, mock strategies, and reusable code:

---

### 1. TRAINING SERVICE STRUCTURE

#### Main Entry Point: `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/services/training-svc/src/training_svc/routers/training.py`

**Key Functions:**

- `_run_hypernetwork_job(job_id, adapter_id, trajectory_id, task_type)`: Background worker that:
  - Takes 4 arguments (as documented in memory)
  - Loads trajectory via `load_trajectory(trajectory_id)`
  - Formats messages via `format_for_sft(trajectory)` 
  - Tokenizes text using `AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_id)`
  - Instantiates `DocToLoraHypernetwork(input_dim=tokenizer.vocab_size)`
  - Loads pretrained weights via `torch.load(hypernetwork_weights_path, map_location="cpu")`
  - Runs forward pass: `weights = hypernetwork(token_ids)` (with `torch.no_grad()`)
  - Saves adapter via `save_hypernetwork_adapter(weights, adapter_dir, base_model_id)`
  - Registers in `AdapterRegistry` with metadata (source="hypernetwork", rank=8, session_id=trajectory_id)
  - Updates `JOB_STORE[job_id]` status to "completed" or "failed"

- `train_hypernetwork(request, background_tasks)`: FastAPI endpoint that:
  - Validates `trajectory_ids` is non-empty (raises 422 if empty)
  - Assigns `adapter_id` at request time (not at job completion)
  - Queues `_run_hypernetwork_job` as background task
  - Returns immediately with `{"job_id": ..., "status": "queued"}`

**Environment Variables Used:**
```python
RUNE_BASE_MODEL = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct" (default)
RUNE_HYPERNETWORK_WEIGHTS_PATH = Path.home() / ".rune" / "hypernetwork.pt" (default)
RUNE_ADAPTER_DIR = optional, defaults to ~/.rune/adapters/
RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR = optional, set by tests via monkeypatch
```

**Thread Safety Pattern:**
- Uses `_JOB_STORE_LOCK = threading.Lock()` from `training_svc.jobs`
- All `JOB_STORE[job_id]` access wrapped with `with _JOB_STORE_LOCK:`

---

### 2. TEST FILES IN `libs/model-training/tests/`

All 6 test files exist (no test_hypernetwork_qwen3.py or test_d2l_data.py):

| File | Lines | Focus |
|------|-------|-------|
| `test_hypernetwork.py` | 297 | DocToLoraHypernetwork unit tests, save_hypernetwork_adapter |
| `test_trainer.py` | 187 | train_qlora, train_and_register importability, trajectory handling |
| `test_trajectory.py` | 183 | load_trajectory, record_trajectory, format_for_sft |
| `test_config.py` | 96 | get_training_config, validate_config validation |
| `test_peft_utils.py` | 60 | build_qlora_config, apply_lora_adapter, merge_adapter |
| `test_merging.py` | 87 | ties_merge, dare_merge, load_adapter_state_dict |

Also:
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/services/training-svc/tests/test_training.py` (233 lines) - integration tests
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/conftest.py` - root fixtures with `_make_factory()` helper

---

### 3. MOCK STRATEGIES & GPU DEPENDENCY INJECTION

#### Pattern: INFRA-05 (Deferred GPU Imports)
All torch, transformers, safetensors, trl imports are INSIDE function bodies, never at module level. This keeps modules importable in CPU-only CI.

#### Test Hypernetwork Mocking (`test_hypernetwork.py`):

1. **Importability test** (lines 20-34):
   ```python
   def test_hypernetwork_importable_without_gpu() -> None:
       from model_training.hypernetwork import DocToLoraHypernetwork
       assert callable(DocToLoraHypernetwork)
   ```

2. **Fake GPU Module Injection** (lines 41-80):
   ```python
   _FAKE_HN_MODULES = ["torch", "torch.nn", "safetensors", "safetensors.torch"]
   
   def _inject_fake_hn_modules() -> None:
       fake_torch = ModuleType("torch")
       fake_torch.Tensor = MagicMock()
       fake_torch.randn = MagicMock()
       # ... build fake_nn, fake_safetensors similarly
       sys.modules.setdefault("torch", fake_torch)
       sys.modules.setdefault("torch.nn", fake_nn)
       sys.modules.setdefault("safetensors", fake_safetensors)
       sys.modules.setdefault("safetensors.torch", fake_safetensors_torch)
   ```

3. **Torch Availability Detection** (lines 86-96):
   ```python
   try:
       import torch
       import torch.nn
       _TORCH_AVAILABLE = not isinstance(torch, MagicMock) and hasattr(torch.nn, "Module")
   except ImportError:
       _TORCH_AVAILABLE = False
   
   requires_torch = pytest.mark.skipif(
       not _TORCH_AVAILABLE, reason="torch not installed in this environment"
   )
   ```

4. **Safetensors Module Ensurer** (lines 184-193):
   ```python
   def _ensure_safetensors_module() -> None:
       if "safetensors" not in sys.modules:
           fake_st = ModuleType("safetensors")
           fake_st_torch = ModuleType("safetensors.torch")
           fake_st_torch.save_file = MagicMock()
           fake_st.torch = fake_st_torch
           sys.modules["safetensors"] = fake_st
           sys.modules["safetensors.torch"] = fake_st_torch
   ```

#### Test Trainer Mocking (`test_trainer.py`):

**Fake GPU Modules List** (lines 131-142):
```python

    "datasets",
```

**Injection Pattern** (lines 145-177):

```python
    fake_torch = ModuleType("torch")
    fake_torch.bfloat16 = MagicMock()
    
    fake_datasets = ModuleType("datasets")
    fake_dataset_cls = MagicMock()

    fake_datasets.Dataset = fake_dataset_cls
    
    fake_transformers = ModuleType("transformers")
    fake_transformers.AutoModelForCausalLM = MagicMock()
    fake_transformers.AutoTokenizer = MagicMock()
    fake_transformers.BitsAndBytesConfig = MagicMock()
    
    fake_trl = ModuleType("trl")
    fake_trl.SFTConfig = MagicMock()
    fake_trl.SFTTrainer = MagicMock()
    
    fake_peft = ModuleType("peft")
    fake_peft.LoraConfig = MagicMock()
    fake_peft.get_peft_model = MagicMock()
    
    sys.modules.setdefault("torch", fake_torch)
    # ... register all others
```

**Removal Pattern** (lines 179-187):
```python
def _remove_fake_gpu_modules() -> None:
    for mod in ("torch", "datasets", "transformers", "trl", "peft"):
        if mod in sys.modules and isinstance(sys.modules[mod], ModuleType):
            m = sys.modules[mod]
            if getattr(m, "__file__", "FAKE") is None:
                del sys.modules[mod]
```

---

### 4. TRAINING SERVICE INTEGRATION TEST (`services/training-svc/tests/test_training.py`)

#### Test Hypernetwork Job Registration (lines 118-233):

**Setup:**
```python
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def clear_job_store():
    from training_svc.jobs import JOB_STORE
    JOB_STORE.clear()
    yield
    JOB_STORE.clear()
```

**Full Integration Flow:**
1. Creates in-memory SQLite test database
2. Pre-populates `JOB_STORE[job_id] = JobStatus(job_id, status="queued", adapter_id)`
3. Injects sys.modules mocks:
   - `torch` with `torch.no_grad()`, `torch.load()` mocks
   - `transformers.AutoTokenizer` returning mock with `vocab_size=32000`
   - `model_training.hypernetwork` with **real** `save_hypernetwork_adapter` (not mocked!)
   - `model_training.trajectory` with mocked `load_trajectory` and `format_for_sft`
4. Calls `_run_hypernetwork_job(job_id, adapter_id, trajectory_id, task_type)`
5. Verifies:
   - `JOB_STORE[job_id].status == "completed"`
   - `AdapterRegistry.retrieve_by_id(adapter_id)` returns record with:
     - `source == "hypernetwork"`
     - `rank == 8`
     - `task_type == task_type`
     - `session_id == trajectory_id`
     - `file_hash` is 64-char SHA-256 hex
     - `file_size_bytes > 0`

**Key Mock Implementation:**

```python
def _make_factory(cls: type[T], defaults: dict[str, Any]) -> Callable[..., T]:
    """Return a factory callable that creates *cls* instances with merged defaults."""
    def _factory(**kwargs: Any) -> T:
        return cls(**{**defaults, **kwargs})
    return _factory

@pytest.fixture
def make_adapter_record() -> Callable[..., AdapterRecord]:
    return _make_factory(
        AdapterRecord,
        {
            "id": "test-adapter-001",
            "version": 1,
            "task_type": "bug-fix",
            "base_model_id": "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
            "rank": 16,
            # ... 10 more fields with deterministic defaults
        },
    )
```

All 7 fixtures use this pattern (make_adapter_record, make_adapter_ref, make_coding_session, make_training_job, make_evolution_job, make_evol_metrics, make_swarm_config, make_swarm_checkpoint).

### 8. PYTEST MARKERS & XFAIL PATTERNS

**skipif markers:**

  - Skips if torch is not installed or is a MagicMock

**xfail markers:**

- `@pytest.mark.xfail(reason="Full GPU mocking chain is fragile in CPU CI", strict=False)` in test_trainer.py

### 9. KEY HYPERNETWORK IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

#### Lazy Proxy Pattern (`hypernetwork.py` lines 210-231):
```python
class _LazyHypernetworkProxy:
    """Lazy proxy for DocToLoraHypernetwork.
    
    Acts as a callable that creates a real nn.Module instance on first call.
    The class is rebuilt (and cached) on first instantiation so that torch is
    only imported when actually needed (INFRA-05 pattern).
    """
    _real_class: type | None = None
    
    def __call__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
        if self._real_class is None:
            self.__class__._real_class = _build_hypernetwork_class()
        return self._real_class(*args, **kwargs)
    
    def __instancecheck__(self, instance: object) -> bool:
        if self._real_class is None:
            return False
        return isinstance(instance, self._real_class)

DocToLoraHypernetwork: _LazyHypernetworkProxy = _LazyHypernetworkProxy()
```

#### Constructor Args:
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    input_dim: int,  # vocab_size from tokenizer
    num_latents: int = 32,
    latent_dim: int = 256,
    depth: int = 4,
    heads: int = 8,
    rank: int = 8,  # Fixed to 8 for hypernetwork
    target_modules: Sequence[str] = ("q_proj", "v_proj"),
    num_layers: int = 28,  # Target model (Qwen2.5-7B)
    hidden_dim: int = 4096,  # Target model hidden dim
    module_dims: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] | None = None,  # For GQA
) -> None:
```

#### forward() Signature:
```python
def forward(self, token_ids: torch.Tensor) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    """Generate PEFT-compatible LoRA adapter weights from token IDs.
    
    Args:
        token_ids: Integer token ID tensor of shape (batch, seq_len).
    
    Returns:
        Dict mapping PEFT state_dict keys to weight tensors.
        Keys follow:
          base_model.model.model.layers.{i}.self_attn.{module}.lora_{A|B}.weight
        Returns first batch element (one adapter per trajectory).
    """
```

### 11. UNUSED/NOT-YET-IMPLEMENTED CODE

**Files that exist but are NOT used in main tests:**
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/src/model_training/sakana_d2l.py` - Sakana D2L integration with flash attention patching (patch_flash_attention, etc.)

- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/src/model_training/peft_utils.py` - LoRA config building

**No test_hypernetwork_qwen3.py or test_d2l_data.py exists** (as noted in the exploration request).

---

### 12. CRUCIAL DESIGN DECISIONS

1. **Hypernetwork rank is fixed to 8** - not configurable per adapter
2. **adapter_id is assigned at request time**, not at job completion (line 276 in training.py)
3. **No modules_to_save in LoraConfig** - vLLM rejects this (per Phase 21-01 decision)
4. **embed_tokens and lm_head excluded** from target_modules to prevent vLLM conflicts
5. **Token IDs come from base model's tokenizer**, not custom tokenization - hypernetwork learns its own embedding
6. **JOB_STORE is in-memory dict**, not database - status only tracked in RAM during job execution
7. **All GPU imports deferred to function bodies** - module-level imports are stdlib only
8. **Adapter files stored with sha256 hash** of safetensors file for integrity tracking

---

    11→  - Saving the generated LoRA weights in PEFT format

    33→def _patch_flash_attention() -> None:
    34→    """Patch Sakana's idefics2 module to work without flash_attn.
    35→
    36→    Replaces flash attention classes and assertions with eager equivalents
    37→    so the perceiver can run on CPU/MPS/CUDA without flash_attn installed.
    38→    """
    39→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    40→    import torch.nn as nn  # noqa: PLC0415
    41→
    42→    import ctx_to_lora.modeling.idefics2 as idefics2_mod  # noqa: PLC0415
    43→    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.idefics2 import (  # noqa: PLC0415
    44→        Idefics2Perceiver,
    45→        Idefics2PerceiverAttention,
    46→        Idefics2PerceiverConfig,
    47→        Idefics2PerceiverResampler,
    48→    )
    49→
    50→    # Map both eager and flash_attention_2 to the eager attention class
    51→    idefics2_mod.IDEFICS2_PERCEIVER_ATTENTION_CLASSES["eager"] = (
    52→        Idefics2PerceiverAttention
    53→    )
    54→    idefics2_mod.IDEFICS2_PERCEIVER_ATTENTION_CLASSES["flash_attention_2"] = (
    55→        Idefics2PerceiverAttention
    56→    )
    57→
    58→    # Patch eager attention forward to accept flash-only kwargs
    59→    _orig_attn_fwd = Idefics2PerceiverAttention.forward
    60→
    61→    def _patched_attn_fwd(
    62→        self: Any, *args: Any, is_cross_attn: Any = None, **kwargs: Any
    63→    ) -> Any:
    64→        kwargs.pop("cu_seq_lens_q", None)
    65→        kwargs.pop("cu_seq_lens_k", None)
    66→        kwargs.pop("max_length_q", None)
    67→        kwargs.pop("max_length_k", None)
    68→        return _orig_attn_fwd(self, *args, **kwargs)
    69→
    70→    Idefics2PerceiverAttention.forward = _patched_attn_fwd
    71→
    72→    # Patch resampler forward to use eager path
    73→    def _eager_resampler_forward(
    74→        self: Any,
    75→        context: torch.Tensor,
    76→        attention_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    77→        position_ids: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    78→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
    79→        if position_ids is None:
    80→            bsz = context.shape[0]
    81→        else:
    82→            bsz = torch.where(position_ids == 0, 1, 0).sum().item()
    83→        latents = self.latents_q.unsqueeze(0).expand((bsz, *self.latents_q.size()))
    84→        compressed_context = latents
    85→        for layer in self.layers:
    86→            layer_outputs = layer(
    87→                latents=compressed_context,
    88→                context=context,
    89→                attention_mask=None,
    90→                position_ids=None,
    91→                past_key_value=None,
    92→                output_attentions=False,
    93→                use_cache=False,
    94→            )
    95→            compressed_context = layer_outputs[0]
    96→        return self.layernorm(compressed_context)
    97→

   134→def download_checkpoint(
   135→    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
   136→) -> Path:
   137→    """Download Sakana's pretrained checkpoint from HuggingFace.
   138→
   139→    Args:
   140→        variant: Which checkpoint variant to download.
   141→            Options: 'gemma_demo', 'gemma_2b_d2l', 'mistral_7b_d2l', 'qwen_4b_d2l'.
   142→
   143→    Returns:
   144→        Path to the downloaded checkpoint file.
   145→    """
   146→    # Determine filename based on variant
   147→    if variant == "gemma_demo":
   148→        hf_filename = "gemma_demo/checkpoint-80000/pytorch_model.bin"
   149→    elif variant in ("gemma_2b_d2l", "mistral_7b_d2l", "qwen_4b_d2l"):
   150→        hf_filename = f"{variant}/checkpoint-20000/pytorch_model.bin"
   151→    else:
   152→        msg = f"Unknown variant: {variant}"
   153→        raise ValueError(msg)
   154→
   155→    cached = LOCAL_CACHE_DIR / variant / "pytorch_model.bin"
   156→    if cached.exists():
   157→        logger.info("Using cached Sakana checkpoint: %s", cached)
   158→        return cached
   159→
   160→    from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download  # noqa: PLC0415
   161→
   162→    logger.info("Downloading Sakana checkpoint %s from %s...", variant, HF_REPO_ID)
   163→    downloaded = Path(hf_hub_download(repo_id=HF_REPO_ID, filename=hf_filename))
   164→
   165→    cached.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   166→    import shutil  # noqa: PLC0415
   167→
   168→    shutil.copy2(downloaded, cached)
   169→    logger.info("Cached to: %s", cached)
   170→    return cached
   171→
   172→
   173→def load_sakana_checkpoint(
   174→    checkpoint_path: str | Path | None = None,
   175→    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
   176→    device: str = "cpu",
   177→) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
   178→    """Load Sakana's HyperLoRA perceiver from checkpoint.
   179→
   180→    Downloads from HuggingFace if no local path is provided.
   181→    Patches flash attention for CPU/MPS compatibility.
   182→
   183→    Args:
   184→        checkpoint_path: Path to local checkpoint. If None, downloads from HF.
   185→        variant: HF checkpoint variant (only used if checkpoint_path is None).
   186→        device: Device to load onto.
   187→
   188→    Returns:
   189→        Tuple of (hypernet, hypernet_config).
   190→    """
   191→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   192→
   193→    _patch_flash_attention()
   194→
   195→    if checkpoint_path is None:
   196→        checkpoint_path = download_checkpoint(variant)
   197→
   198→    logger.info("Loading Sakana checkpoint: %s", checkpoint_path)
   199→    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   200→
   201→    hc = sd["hypernet_config"]
   202→    logger.info(
   203→        "HypernetConfig: latent_size=%d, lora_r=%d, base_model=%s",
   204→        hc.latent_size,
   205→        hc.lora_config.r,
   206→        sd["base_model_name_or_path"],
   207→    )
   208→
   209→    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415
   210→
   211→    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)
   212→
   213→    # Load perceiver/aggregator/head weights from checkpoint
   214→    hypernet_keys = [
   215→        k for k in sd.keys() if k.startswith(("aggregator.", "head.", "extra_"))
   216→    ]
   217→    hypernet_sd = {k: sd[k] for k in hypernet_keys if k in hypernet.state_dict()}
   218→
   219→    if hypernet_sd:
   220→        loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(hypernet_sd, strict=False)
   221→        logger.info("Loaded %d perceiver weight tensors", len(hypernet_sd))
   222→        if loaded.missing_keys:
   223→            logger.info("Missing keys: %d", len(loaded.missing_keys))
   224→    else:
   225→        full_state = {k: v for k, v in sd.items() if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor)}
   226→        loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(full_state, strict=False)
   227→        logger.info(
   228→            "Loaded full state: missing=%d, unexpected=%d",
   229→            len(loaded.missing_keys),
   230→            len(loaded.unexpected_keys),
   231→        )
   232→
   233→    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)
   234→    hypernet.eval()
   235→
   236→    param_count = sum(p.numel() for p in hypernet.parameters())
   237→    logger.info("HyperLoRA params: %d", param_count)
   238→
   239→    return hypernet, hc
   240→
   241→
   242→def extract_activations(
   243→    text: str,
   244→    base_model_name: str,
   245→    layer_indices: list[int],
   246→    device: str = "cpu",
   247→    max_length: int = 512,
   248→) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
   249→    """Extract per-layer hidden state activations from the base model.
   250→
   251→    Runs the input text through the base model and captures hidden states
   252→    at the specified layer indices. These activations are what Sakana's
   253→    HyperLoRA perceiver expects as input.
   254→
   255→    Args:
   256→        text: Input text to process.
   257→        base_model_name: HuggingFace model ID for the base model.
   258→        layer_indices: Which layers to extract activations from.
   259→        device: Device for computation.
   260→        max_length: Max token sequence length.
   261→
   262→    Returns:
   263→        Tuple of (features, attention_mask) ready for HyperLoRA.
   264→        features shape: (1, num_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)
   265→        attention_mask shape: (1, seq_len)
   266→    """
   267→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   268→    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415
   269→
   270→    logger.info("Loading base model %s for activation extraction...", base_model_name)
   271→    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_name)
   272→    model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
   273→        base_model_name,
   274→        output_hidden_states=True,
   275→        torch_dtype=torch.float32,
   276→    )
   277→    model.to(device)
   278→    model.eval()
   279→
   280→    inputs = tokenizer(
   281→        text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=True, max_length=max_length
   282→    )
   283→    inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in inputs.items()}
   284→
   285→    with torch.no_grad():
   286→        outputs = model(**inputs)
   287→
   288→    # outputs.hidden_states is a tuple of (num_layers+1,) tensors
   289→    # each of shape (batch, seq_len, hidden_dim)
   290→    hidden_states = outputs.hidden_states
   291→
   292→    # Stack selected layers: (batch, num_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)
   293→    selected = torch.stack([hidden_states[i] for i in layer_indices], dim=1)
   294→    attention_mask = inputs["attention_mask"]
   295→
   296→    logger.info(
   297→        "Extracted activations: %s from %d layers", selected.shape, len(layer_indices)
   298→    )
   299→
   300→    # Free base model memory
   301→    del model
   302→    if device != "cpu":
   303→        torch.cuda.empty_cache() if "cuda" in device else None
   304→
   305→    return selected, attention_mask
   306→
   307→
   308→def generate_adapter_from_sakana(
   309→    text: str,
   310→    output_dir: str,
   311→    checkpoint_path: str | Path | None = None,
   312→    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
   313→    base_model_name: str | None = None,
   314→    device: str = "cpu",
   315→) -> str:
   316→    """End-to-end: text → base model activations → HyperLoRA → PEFT adapter.
   317→
   318→    This is the main entry point. It:
   319→      1. Loads the Sakana pretrained perceiver (downloading if needed)
   320→      2. Runs text through the base model to get per-layer activations
   321→      3. Feeds activations through the perceiver to generate LoRA weights
   322→      4. Saves weights in PEFT-compatible format
   323→
   324→    Args:
   325→        text: Input text (trajectory, document, context) to encode.
   326→        output_dir: Directory to save the PEFT adapter files.
   327→        checkpoint_path: Path to local checkpoint, or None to download.
   328→        variant: HF checkpoint variant if downloading.
   329→        base_model_name: Override base model. If None, uses the one from checkpoint.
   330→        device: Device for computation.
   331→
   332→    Returns:
   333→        Path to the saved adapter directory.
   334→    """
   335→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   336→
   337→    hypernet, hc = load_sakana_checkpoint(checkpoint_path, variant, device)
   338→
   339→    # Determine base model from checkpoint config
   340→    if base_model_name is None:
   341→        # Load checkpoint just to read base_model_name_or_path
   342→        if checkpoint_path is None:
   343→            checkpoint_path = download_checkpoint(variant)
   344→        sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   345→        base_model_name = sd["base_model_name_or_path"]
   346→        del sd
   347→
   348→    logger.info("Base model: %s", base_model_name)
   349→
   350→    # Extract activations from base model
   351→    layer_indices = list(hc.layer_indices)
   352→    features, attn_mask = extract_activations(
   353→        text=text,
   354→        base_model_name=base_model_name,
   355→        layer_indices=layer_indices,
   356→        device=device,
   357→        max_length=512,
   358→    )
   359→
   360→    # Generate LoRA weights via perceiver
   361→    logger.info("Generating LoRA weights via HyperLoRA perceiver...")
   362→    with torch.no_grad():
   363→        lora_dict, layernorm_dict = hypernet.generate_weights(
   364→            features, attn_mask, None
   365→        )
   366→
   367→    # Save as PEFT adapter
   368→    _save_sakana_adapter(
   369→        lora_dict=lora_dict,
   370→        output_dir=output_dir,
   371→        base_model_name=base_model_name,
   372→        hc=hc,
   373→    )
   374→
   375→    return output_dir
   376→
   377→
   378→def _save_sakana_adapter(
   379→    lora_dict: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
   380→    output_dir: str,
   381→    base_model_name: str,
   382→    hc: Any,
   383→) -> None:
   384→    """Save Sakana's HyperLoRA output as a PEFT-compatible adapter.
   385→
   386→    Converts from Sakana's format (dict of module → {A, B} with batch/layer dims)
   387→    to PEFT's flat state_dict format.
   388→
   389→    Args:
   390→        lora_dict: Output from HyperLoRA.generate_weights().
   391→        output_dir: Directory to write adapter files.
   392→        base_model_name: Base model identifier for adapter config.
   393→        hc: HypernetConfig with lora rank and target modules.
   394→    """

"""SakanaAI Doc-to-LoRA integration.

Wraps Sakana's pretrained HyperLoRA perceiver so it can be used through
our hypernetwork interface (load_pretrained → generate_adapter).

The Sakana hypernetwork takes per-layer activations from a base model as
input and produces LoRA adapter weights.  This module handles:
  - Downloading the checkpoint from HuggingFace
  - Patching flash-attention assertions for CPU/MPS/non-flash environments
  - Extracting per-layer activations from the base model
  - Saving the generated LoRA weights in PEFT format

GPU imports are deferred inside function bodies per INFRA-05 pattern.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# HuggingFace repo for Sakana's pretrained checkpoints
HF_REPO_ID = "SakanaAI/doc-to-lora"
# Available checkpoints: gemma_2b_d2l, gemma_demo, mistral_7b_d2l, qwen_4b_d2l
DEFAULT_VARIANT = "gemma_demo"
DEFAULT_HF_FILENAME = f"{DEFAULT_VARIANT}/checkpoint-80000/pytorch_model.bin"
LOCAL_CACHE_DIR = Path.home() / ".cache" / "rune" / "sakana_d2l"


def _patch_flash_attention() -> None:
    """Patch Sakana's idefics2 module to work without flash_attn.

    Replaces flash attention classes and assertions with eager equivalents
    so the perceiver can run on CPU/MPS/CUDA without flash_attn installed.
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    import torch.nn as nn  # noqa: PLC0415

    import ctx_to_lora.modeling.idefics2 as idefics2_mod  # noqa: PLC0415
    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.idefics2 import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        Idefics2Perceiver,
        Idefics2PerceiverAttention,
        Idefics2PerceiverConfig,
        Idefics2PerceiverResampler,
    )

    # Map both eager and flash_attention_2 to the eager attention class
    idefics2_mod.IDEFICS2_PERCEIVER_ATTENTION_CLASSES["eager"] = (
        Idefics2PerceiverAttention
    )
    idefics2_mod.IDEFICS2_PERCEIVER_ATTENTION_CLASSES["flash_attention_2"] = (
        Idefics2PerceiverAttention
    )

    # Patch eager attention forward to accept flash-only kwargs
    _orig_attn_fwd = Idefics2PerceiverAttention.forward

    def _patched_attn_fwd(
        self: Any, *args: Any, is_cross_attn: Any = None, **kwargs: Any
    ) -> Any:
        kwargs.pop("cu_seq_lens_q", None)
        kwargs.pop("cu_seq_lens_k", None)
        kwargs.pop("max_length_q", None)
        kwargs.pop("max_length_k", None)
        return _orig_attn_fwd(self, *args, **kwargs)

    Idefics2PerceiverAttention.forward = _patched_attn_fwd

    # Patch resampler forward to use eager path
    def _eager_resampler_forward(
        self: Any,
        context: torch.Tensor,
        attention_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
        position_ids: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        if position_ids is None:
            bsz = context.shape[0]
        else:
            bsz = torch.where(position_ids == 0, 1, 0).sum().item()
        latents = self.latents_q.unsqueeze(0).expand((bsz, *self.latents_q.size()))
        compressed_context = latents
        for layer in self.layers:
            layer_outputs = layer(
                latents=compressed_context,
                context=context,
                attention_mask=None,
                position_ids=None,
                past_key_value=None,
                output_attentions=False,
                use_cache=False,
            )
            compressed_context = layer_outputs[0]
        return self.layernorm(compressed_context)

    Idefics2PerceiverResampler.forward = _eager_resampler_forward

    # Patch resampler __init__ to bypass flash_attention_2 assertion
    _orig_resampler_init = Idefics2PerceiverResampler.__init__

    def _patched_resampler_init(self: Any, config: Any) -> None:
        config._attn_implementation = "flash_attention_2"
        _orig_resampler_init(self, config)
        self._use_flash_attention_2 = False

    Idefics2PerceiverResampler.__init__ = _patched_resampler_init

    # Patch Perceiver init to force eager on sub-configs
    _orig_perceiver_init = Idefics2Perceiver.__init__

    def _patched_perceiver_init(self: Any, enc_cfg: Any, dec_cfg: Any) -> None:
        enc_cfg._attn_implementation = "eager"
        enc_cfg._attn_implementation_internal = "eager"
        dec_cfg._attn_implementation = "eager"
        dec_cfg._attn_implementation_internal = "eager"
        _orig_perceiver_init(self, enc_cfg, dec_cfg)

    Idefics2Perceiver.__init__ = _patched_perceiver_init

    # Patch PerceiverConfig init to default to eager
    _orig_cfg_init = Idefics2PerceiverConfig.__init__

    def _patched_cfg_init(self: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        kwargs["attn_implementation"] = "eager"
        _orig_cfg_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
        self._attn_implementation = "eager"
        self._attn_implementation_internal = "eager"

    Idefics2PerceiverConfig.__init__ = _patched_cfg_init


def download_checkpoint(
    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
) -> Path:
    """Download Sakana's pretrained checkpoint from HuggingFace.

    Args:
        variant: Which checkpoint variant to download.
            Options: 'gemma_demo', 'gemma_2b_d2l', 'mistral_7b_d2l', 'qwen_4b_d2l'.

    Returns:
        Path to the downloaded checkpoint file.
    """
    # Determine filename based on variant
    if variant == "gemma_demo":
        hf_filename = "gemma_demo/checkpoint-80000/pytorch_model.bin"
    elif variant in ("gemma_2b_d2l", "mistral_7b_d2l", "qwen_4b_d2l"):
        hf_filename = f"{variant}/checkpoint-20000/pytorch_model.bin"
    else:
        msg = f"Unknown variant: {variant}"
        raise ValueError(msg)

    cached = LOCAL_CACHE_DIR / variant / "pytorch_model.bin"
    if cached.exists():
        logger.info("Using cached Sakana checkpoint: %s", cached)
        return cached

    from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download  # noqa: PLC0415

    logger.info("Downloading Sakana checkpoint %s from %s...", variant, HF_REPO_ID)
    downloaded = Path(hf_hub_download(repo_id=HF_REPO_ID, filename=hf_filename))

    cached.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    import shutil  # noqa: PLC0415

    shutil.copy2(downloaded, cached)
    logger.info("Cached to: %s", cached)
    return cached


def load_sakana_checkpoint(
    checkpoint_path: str | Path | None = None,
    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
    device: str = "cpu",
) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
    """Load Sakana's HyperLoRA perceiver from checkpoint.

    Downloads from HuggingFace if no local path is provided.
    Patches flash attention for CPU/MPS compatibility.

    Args:
        checkpoint_path: Path to local checkpoint. If None, downloads from HF.
        variant: HF checkpoint variant (only used if checkpoint_path is None).
        device: Device to load onto.

    Returns:
        Tuple of (hypernet, hypernet_config).
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415

    _patch_flash_attention()

    if checkpoint_path is None:
        checkpoint_path = download_checkpoint(variant)

    logger.info("Loading Sakana checkpoint: %s", checkpoint_path)
    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)

    hc = sd["hypernet_config"]
    logger.info(
        "HypernetConfig: latent_size=%d, lora_r=%d, base_model=%s",
        hc.latent_size,
        hc.lora_config.r,
        sd["base_model_name_or_path"],
    )

    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415

    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)

    # Load perceiver/aggregator/head weights from checkpoint
    hypernet_keys = [
        k for k in sd.keys() if k.startswith(("aggregator.", "head.", "extra_"))
    ]
    hypernet_sd = {k: sd[k] for k in hypernet_keys if k in hypernet.state_dict()}

    if hypernet_sd:
        loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(hypernet_sd, strict=False)
        logger.info("Loaded %d perceiver weight tensors", len(hypernet_sd))
        if loaded.missing_keys:
            logger.info("Missing keys: %d", len(loaded.missing_keys))
    else:
        full_state = {k: v for k, v in sd.items() if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor)}
        loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(full_state, strict=False)
        logger.info(
            "Loaded full state: missing=%d, unexpected=%d",
            len(loaded.missing_keys),
            len(loaded.unexpected_keys),
        )

    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)
    hypernet.eval()

    param_count = sum(p.numel() for p in hypernet.parameters())
    logger.info("HyperLoRA params: %d", param_count)

    return hypernet, hc


def extract_activations(
    text: str,
    base_model_name: str,
    layer_indices: list[int],
    device: str = "cpu",
    max_length: int = 512,
) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
    """Extract per-layer hidden state activations from the base model.

    Runs the input text through the base model and captures hidden states
    at the specified layer indices. These activations are what Sakana's
    HyperLoRA perceiver expects as input.

    Args:
        text: Input text to process.
        base_model_name: HuggingFace model ID for the base model.
        layer_indices: Which layers to extract activations from.
        device: Device for computation.
        max_length: Max token sequence length.

    Returns:
        Tuple of (features, attention_mask) ready for HyperLoRA.
        features shape: (1, num_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)
        attention_mask shape: (1, seq_len)
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415

    logger.info("Loading base model %s for activation extraction...", base_model_name)
    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_name)
    model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
        base_model_name,
        output_hidden_states=True,
        torch_dtype=torch.float32,
    )
    model.to(device)
    model.eval()

    inputs = tokenizer(
        text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=True, max_length=max_length
    )
    inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in inputs.items()}

    with torch.no_grad():
        outputs = model(**inputs)

    # outputs.hidden_states is a tuple of (num_layers+1,) tensors
    # each of shape (batch, seq_len, hidden_dim)
    hidden_states = outputs.hidden_states

    # Stack selected layers: (batch, num_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)
    selected = torch.stack([hidden_states[i] for i in layer_indices], dim=1)
    attention_mask = inputs["attention_mask"]

    logger.info(
        "Extracted activations: %s from %d layers", selected.shape, len(layer_indices)
    )

    # Free base model memory
    del model
    if device != "cpu":
        torch.cuda.empty_cache() if "cuda" in device else None

    return selected, attention_mask


def generate_adapter_from_sakana(
    text: str,
    output_dir: str,
    checkpoint_path: str | Path | None = None,
    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
    base_model_name: str | None = None,
    device: str = "cpu",
) -> str:
    """End-to-end: text → base model activations → HyperLoRA → PEFT adapter.

    This is the main entry point. It:
      1. Loads the Sakana pretrained perceiver (downloading if needed)
      2. Runs text through the base model to get per-layer activations
      3. Feeds activations through the perceiver to generate LoRA weights
      4. Saves weights in PEFT-compatible format

    Args:
        text: Input text (trajectory, document, context) to encode.
        output_dir: Directory to save the PEFT adapter files.
        checkpoint_path: Path to local checkpoint, or None to download.
        variant: HF checkpoint variant if downloading.
        base_model_name: Override base model. If None, uses the one from checkpoint.
        device: Device for computation.

    Returns:
        Path to the saved adapter directory.
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415

    hypernet, hc = load_sakana_checkpoint(checkpoint_path, variant, device)

    # Determine base model from checkpoint config
    if base_model_name is None:
        # Load checkpoint just to read base_model_name_or_path
        if checkpoint_path is None:
            checkpoint_path = download_checkpoint(variant)
        sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
        base_model_name = sd["base_model_name_or_path"]
        del sd

    logger.info("Base model: %s", base_model_name)

    # Extract activations from base model
    layer_indices = list(hc.layer_indices)
    features, attn_mask = extract_activations(
        text=text,
        base_model_name=base_model_name,
        layer_indices=layer_indices,
        device=device,
        max_length=512,
    )

    # Generate LoRA weights via perceiver
    logger.info("Generating LoRA weights via HyperLoRA perceiver...")
    with torch.no_grad():
        lora_dict, layernorm_dict = hypernet.generate_weights(
            features, attn_mask, None
        )

    # Save as PEFT adapter
    _save_sakana_adapter(
        lora_dict=lora_dict,
        output_dir=output_dir,
        base_model_name=base_model_name,
        hc=hc,
    )

    return output_dir


def _save_sakana_adapter(
    lora_dict: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
    output_dir: str,
    base_model_name: str,
    hc: Any,
) -> None:
    """Save Sakana's HyperLoRA output as a PEFT-compatible adapter.

    Converts from Sakana's format (dict of module → {A, B} with batch/layer dims)
    to PEFT's flat state_dict format.

    Args:
        lora_dict: Output from HyperLoRA.generate_weights().
        output_dir: Directory to write adapter files.
        base_model_name: Base model identifier for adapter config.
        hc: HypernetConfig with lora rank and target modules.
    """
    from safetensors.torch import save_file  # noqa: PLC0415

    output_path = Path(output_dir)
    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    layer_indices = list(hc.layer_indices)
    target_modules = list(hc.lora_config.target_modules)
    rank = hc.lora_config.r

    # Convert Sakana format → PEFT flat state_dict
    # Sakana: lora_dict[module_name]["A"] shape (batch, num_layers, rank, in_features)
    # PEFT: base_model.model.model.layers.{i}.mlp.{module}.lora_A.weight (rank, in_features)
    state_dict = {}
    for mod_name, weights in lora_dict.items():
        if mod_name not in target_modules:
            continue
        a_weights = weights["A"]  # (batch, num_layers, rank, in_features)
        b_weights = weights["B"]  # (batch, num_layers, rank, out_features)

        for layer_pos, layer_idx in enumerate(layer_indices):
            key_a = (
                f"base_model.model.model.layers.{layer_idx}"
                f".mlp.{mod_name}.lora_A.weight"
            )
            key_b = (
                f"base_model.model.model.layers.{layer_idx}"
                f".mlp.{mod_name}.lora_B.weight"
            )
            # Take first batch element, transpose B to (out_features, rank)
            state_dict[key_a] = a_weights[0, layer_pos].contiguous()
            state_dict[key_b] = b_weights[0, layer_pos].t().contiguous()

    save_file(state_dict, str(output_path / "adapter_model.safetensors"))

    adapter_config = {
        "peft_type": "LORA",
        "r": rank,
        "lora_alpha": rank * 2,
        "target_modules": target_modules,
        "lora_dropout": 0.0,
        "bias": "none",
        "task_type": "CAUSAL_LM",
        "base_model_name_or_path": base_model_name,
        "inference_mode": True,
        "modules_to_save": None,
        "fan_in_fan_out": False,
    }
    (output_path / "adapter_config.json").write_text(json.dumps(adapter_config, indent=2))

    logger.info(
        "Saved PEFT adapter: %d tensors, %d layers, rank=%d, targets=%s",
        len(state_dict),
        len(layer_indices),
        rank,
        target_modules,
    )

Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me now identify all the issues with the instruction document:

## Critical Issues Found

### 1. "Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next" doesn't exist
The instructions reference `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next` as the target model. This model doesn't exist on HuggingFace. The instructions say "Before writing any hard-coded values, load the model config programmatically" — but this will fail if the model doesn't exist. This is a blocker. We need to either:
- Use a real model that exists (e.g., `Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-0.6B` or similar)


- Use a placeholder and make it configurable

### 2. Latent dim mismatch: 256 vs 512
The instructions specify a 512-dimensional internal dimension for the Perceiver with `latent_dim: int = 512`, but the current implementation defaults to 256. The qwen_4b_d2l checkpoint from Sakana was likely trained with 512 for HyperLoRA, while the custom DocToLoraHypernetwork uses 256, creating an inconsistency that needs to be resolved.

There's a deeper architectural mismatch here — the Sakana checkpoint uses Idefics2Perceiver with 8 cross-attention blocks and 512-dim internals, while the current DocToLoraHypernetwork has only a single MultiheadAttention layer for cross-attention and 256-dim dimensions. The weight transfer strategy assumes compatibility between these fundamentally different architectures, which won't work directly.

Beyond that, there's an inconsistency in the environment variable naming — the instructions introduce `RUNE_HYPERNETWORK_CHECKPOINT` but the existing codebase uses `RUNE_HYPERNETWORK_WEIGHTS_PATH`. And the custom hypernetwork's single cross-attention layer doesn't match the 8 cross-attention blocks specified in the instructions.

The real issue is that the instructions are mixing two separate implementations: they reference the Sakana checkpoint and its Perceiver architecture with multiple cross-attention blocks, but the actual custom `DocToLoraHypernetwork` has a much simpler design with just one cross-attention layer followed by an encoder stack and linear projection. These are fundamentally different architectures that can't be reconciled.

Beyond that, there's a key format mismatch — the instructions want to switch LoRA targets from `self_attn` modules to `mlp.down_proj`, but the PEFT weight key structure would need to be updated accordingly to reflect this change in the model's layer hierarchy.

Looking at the existing `sakana_d2l.py`, it already handles most of what's being asked — extracting hidden states, using the perceiver to generate LoRA weights, and saving in PEFT format. The instructions essentially want this integrated into the custom `DocToLoraHypernetwork`, but that has a fundamentally different architecture that makes direct porting difficult.

The weight head interpolation approach for handling the dimension change also seems problematic and may not preserve the learned relationships effectively.

There's also a mismatch between the new JSONL data format the instructions define and the existing trajectory format used in the codebase — they're incompatible structures. And while the `inject_lora_adapter` context manager concept is sound, the instructions reference using PEFT's approach, which may not align with how the custom hypernetwork is designed to work.

The current `config.py` doesn't have the hypernetwork configuration function the instructions want to add, though adding `get_d2l_hypernetwork_config()` alongside the existing QLoRA training config should be straightforward. There's also a dependency issue where `generate_code_doc_dataset` relies on the `codeparrot/github-code` dataset, which may not be available or properly configured.

The instructions mention a `--chunk-size` CLI argument but never explain what it does in the training loop. More critically, the plan to replace `token_embedding` with `modality_proj` while maintaining backward compatibility creates a signature mismatch—the forward method would need to accept both `token_ids` and `hidden_states`, but there's no guidance on handling the resulting dimension incompatibility.

I also notice that `extract_hidden_states` is being proposed as a new helper when `extract_activations` in sakana_d2l.py already provides similar functionality with a slightly different interface.

Now I'm seeing several more critical issues to address: the weight transfer strategy needs clarification since Sakana weights can't be directly copied into the custom architecture, I should standardize on either `RUNE_HYPERNETWORK_WEIGHTS_PATH` or `RUNE_HYPERNETWORK_CHECKPOINT` for consistency, I can reuse the existing `extract_activations` function instead of duplicating it, the weight_head interpolation needs a concrete approach, and I need to add a proper cleanup strategy for the LoRA adapter injection. Rather than editing the instructions directly, I'll document all these issues and revisions in a plan file first.
